Question:  The number of geometrical isomer for CH<sub>3</sub> – CH = CH – CH = CH – CH = CH<sub>2</sub>
Options: ["2","4","6","8"]
Answer: ["B"]
Solution:  No. of Geometrical isomers = 2<sup>n</sup><div><p>Molecule have two double bonds that showing geometrical isomerism.</p><p>So total G.I.= 4</p></div>

Question:  A sound source emits frequency of 180 Hz when moving towards a rigid wall with speed 5 m/s and an observer is moving away from wall with speed 5 m/s.  Both source and observer move on a straight line which is perpendicular to the wall.  The number of beats per second heard by the observer will be   (speed of sound = 355 m/s, source is in between observer and wall):
Options: ["5 /s","10 /s","6 /s","8 /s"]
Answer: ["A"]
Solution: <p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="width: 25%;"/></p><p>\[{f_{wall}} = f\left[ {\frac{{355}}{{355 - 5}}} \right] = \frac{{355}}{{350}}f\]</p><p>\[{f_1} = {f_{obs,\,wall}} = {f_{wall}}\left[ {\frac{{355 - 5}}{{355}}} \right] = f = 180Hz\]</p><p>\[{f_{obs,\,source}} = f\left[ {\frac{{355 - 5}}{{355 + 5}}} \right] = \frac{{350}}{{360}}f\]</p><p>\[{f_2} = {f_{obs,\,source}} = 175\,Hz\]</p><p>Beats\[ = {f_1} - {f_2} = 5Hz\]</p>

Question: <b> </b>Compare rate of dehydration of (i), (ii) and (iii) by conc. H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>.<br/><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt=""/>
Options: ["(i) > (ii) > (iii)","(i) > (iii) > (ii)","(ii) > (i) > (iii)","(ii) > (iii) > (ii)"]
Answer: ["A"]
Solution:  due to resonance stabilization<p>(i) Carbocation stabilised by resonance that to extended conjugation.</p><p>(ii) Stabilised by resonance but not by extended conjugation</p><p>(iii) Carbocation will be formed on sp2 carbon which is highly unstable</p><div><br/></div>

Question: <b> </b>Select R-isomers from the following<br><img  src="https://cmds-test.vedantu.com/prod/question-sets/a4e98fbd-0623-498d-bfd8-8ef962af191c1965191874532739228.png"/>
Options: ["I only","II only","I and II only","<p>III Only</p>"]
Answer: ["C"]
Solution: Using the Cahn-Ingold-Prelog (CIP) priority rules:<br>Structure I (Glyceraldehyde): Priority: $-\text{OH}\left( 1 \right)>-\text{CHO}\left( 2 \right)>-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{OH}\left( 3 \right)>$ -H (4). The path $1\to 2\to 3$ is clockwise. Since -H is on the horizontal bond, the configuration is reversed from $S$ to $R$.<br>Structure II (2-chlorobutane): Priority: $-\text{Cl}\left( 1 \right)>-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\left( 2 \right)>-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\left( 3 \right)>$ -H (4). The path $1\to 2\to 3$ is counter-clockwise. Since -H is horizontal, it is reversed to R .<br>Structure III (Alanine): Priority: $-\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\left( 1 \right)>-\text{COOH}\left( 2 \right)>-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\left( 3 \right)>-\text{H}\left( 4 \right)$. Path $1\to 2\to 3$ is counter-clockwise. Since -H is vertical, the configuration remains S .

Question:  Which among the following pairs are diastereomers?<br>(A) <sub></sub><img  src="https://cmds-test.vedantu.com/prod/question-sets/059ea2b7-56e7-4f1e-b883-f57b6654f5cb4749713162650342170.png"/><br>(B) <sub></sub><img  src="https://cmds-test.vedantu.com/prod/question-sets/db7899eb-9863-487a-9c5a-453c69abfe181394399837597317814.png"/><br>(C) <sub></sub><img  src="https://cmds-test.vedantu.com/prod/question-sets/90209913-6222-4464-8aaa-431bc09bea734977940027995317136.png"/><br>(D) <sub></sub><img  src="https://cmds-test.vedantu.com/prod/question-sets/dc183a6a-c936-48c6-9d76-e93b339695a2755483044025676164.png"/><br>Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
Options: [" only (B)"," only (A)"," (A) and (B)"," (A), (B) and (D)"]
Answer: ["D"]
Solution:  <div style='display:inline-block;margin-left:34px' class='qr-indent'>(A), (B) and (D) Configurational isomers which are not mirror images of each other are diastereomers</div>

Question:  Match List I (Reactions/Tests) with List II (Reagents/Products):<br/><div class="uploaded-question-table-par"><table class="uploaded-question-table"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>List I (Reaction Name / Test)</td><td></td><td>List II (Reagents or Observations)</td></tr><tr><td>A.</td><td>Reimer-Tiemann Reaction</td><td>I</td><td>${{I}_{2}}/\text{NaOH}$ (Yellow precipitate)</td></tr><tr><td>B.</td><td>Lucas Test</td><td>II</td><td>$\text{CHC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}/$ aq. NaOH</td></tr><tr><td>C.</td><td>lodoform Test</td><td>III</td><td>Neutral $\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}$ (Violet coloration)</td></tr><tr><td>D.</td><td>Phenol identification</td><td>IV</td><td>Anhydrous $\text{ZnC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+$ conc. HCl</td></tr></tbody></table></div>Select the correct option:
Options: ["A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III","A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II","A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III","A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I"]
Answer: ["A"]
Solution:  <b>A - (II): Reimer-Tiemann Reaction</b><br/>When phenol is treated with chloroform ( $\text{CHC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}$ ) in the presence of sodium hydroxide, an -CHO group is introduced at the ortho position, forming Salicylaldehyde.<div><img src="data:image/png;base64," alt=""/><br/><b>B - (IV): Lucas Test</b><br/>This is used to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols. The reagent is a mixture of anhydrous $\text{ZnC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$ and conc. HCl . Tertiary alcohols produce turbidity immediately.<br/>C - (I): lodoform Test<br/>Used for compounds containing the $\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{CH}\left( \text{OH} \right)$ - or $\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{CO}$ - group (like Ethanol or Acetone). Treating them with ${{I}_{2}}$ and NaOH yields a characteristic yellow precipitate of triiodomethane $\left( \text{CH}{{\text{I}}_{3}} \right)$.<br/>D - (III): Phenol Identification<br/>Phenols react with Neutral Ferric Chloride ( $\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}$ ) to form a complex that gives a deep violet or purple coloration, a classic laboratory diagnostic test.</div>
